seoTitle: Frankston South vs Freeburgh — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Frankston South and Freeburgh. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Frankston South vs Freeburgh: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frankston South and Freeburgh.
heading: Frankston South vs Freeburgh — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frankston South, Victoria and Freeburgh, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freeburgh has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Frankston South (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frankston South or Freeburgh safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frankston South has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Freeburgh scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frankston South scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Freeburgh scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frankston South or Freeburgh?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freeburgh has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
